goal: Make agent behavior visible and measurable: rich synthetic data, a labeled eval set, a scoring runner, and a multi-turn chat UI. Fix embedder load time first.

rules.isolation_invariant: every customer read — SQL and vector — is scoped to a single customer

status_legend.blocked: Cannot continue until a documented blocker resolves.

features.title: load the embedder once; silence import warnings
features.user_visible_behavior: The MiniLM embedder loads a single time per process (cached), so messages after the first are fast. Import-time warnings no longer flood the console.
features.notes: Module-level singleton in retrieval.py (load on first use, reuse after); in app.py wrap in @st.cache_resource so it survives reruns. Add a narrow warnings.filterwarnings for the torch/sentence-transformers UserWarnings. Test: calling the embedder factory twice returns the same object. Manual: send two messages, second is fast, no warning spam.

features.title: synthetic dataset generator with a realistic long tail
features.user_visible_behavior: scripts/generate_dataset.py builds ~40 synthetic customers (IDs from 2001) with realistic ticket histories — most with 1-2 tickets, a deliberate tail with 10-20 — appended to the seeded DB. The canonical customers 1001-1003 are untouched.
features.notes: Fixed random seed -> reproducible IDs and counts. Generator first runs build_database() (seed), then adds synthetic rows; idempotent (clears customer_id>=2001 before regenerating). Ticket text from templates by category (billing, login, crash, refund, shipping...). Mix of Open/Closed. Tests: at least one customer has >=10 tickets; 1001-1003 unchanged; two runs give identical counts. Note: verify.sh resets the DB to seed-only, so re-run this script before a demo.

features.title: labeled eval cases
features.user_visible_behavior: data/eval_cases.json holds ~15-20 cases, each: customer_id, incoming message, should_retrieve (bool), and expected_ticket_ids (when true). Covers both 'should look up history' and 'self-contained, should not'.
features.notes: Schema-validated. ISOLATION SANITY: every expected_ticket_id must belong to that case's customer_id (a labeling error here would reward a leak). Reference both seed and generated customers; run generate_dataset first so referenced IDs exist.

features.title: scoring runner for retrieval decisions
features.user_visible_behavior: scripts/run_eval.py runs the agent over eval_cases and prints: retrieve-or-not decision accuracy, and retrieval hit rate (expected ticket in top-k). Makes 'does retrieval work?' a number.
features.notes: The agent records which tools it called so the runner can compare to labels (handle_ticket exposes an optional trace of tool calls). SCORING LOGIC is gate-tested with stubbed agent outputs (deterministic). Running against the LIVE model is MANUAL (nondeterministic, network) — like the smoke test, not in verify.sh. Manual evidence: paste the printed report.

features.title: multi-turn chat interface
features.user_visible_behavior: The app uses st.chat_message / st.chat_input. A user holds a back-and-forth with the agent; prior turns persist in the session, so the agent can reference earlier messages in the same conversation.
features.notes: handle_ticket gains an optional conversation history param; the chat UI stores turns in st.session_state and passes them. Test (with stub model): prior turns appear in the messages sent to the model; the tool loop still terminates with history present. UI itself verified manually.

features.title: surface eval results, lock the gate
features.user_visible_behavior: README/PROGRESS shows the latest eval numbers. verify.sh runs the full suite (M1+M2+M2.5 deterministic tests) and exits 0.
features.notes: Manual evidence: (1) eval report with real numbers; (2) a chat demo on a high-history generated customer where the agent CHOOSES to retrieve on a back-reference and does NOT on a self-contained question — the visible-judgment beat.
